human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1017 requirements for campaign-trafficking-specific workflow handling, evidence and confidentiality expectations, advisory, editorial, legal, creative approval, contractual, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, research-summary completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal campaign trafficking execution, incomplete inputs, QA failures, missing completion evidence, confidentiality conflicts, live delivery exceptions, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence facts, conflicting policies, realistic terminology, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed campaign trafficking status, readiness, creative and tag QA, tracking validation, launch proof, delivery exceptions, confidentiality handling, and completion evidence rather than legal, editorial, creative approval, privacy, media strategy, financial, contractual, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes IAB/4A's media-buy terms, IAB Tech Lab ACIF, OpenRTB, VAST, and ads.txt standards, MRC invalid-traffic guidance, FTC advertising guidance, and NAI self-regulatory privacy framework with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported setup or launch, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed claim or creative approval, unresolved delivery discrepancy, and generic treatment of materially different ad channels or platform workflows.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, channel, publisher, platform, ad server, creative-ID, privacy, and authority prerequisites for specialized campaign trafficking execution.

Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, rollback controls, and non-regression validation.
